title: Biest + Progesterone Cream: Compounded Bioidentical HRT
titleHtml: Biest + Progesterone Cream: Compounded Bioidentical HRT.
deck: Estradiol and estriol in an 80/20 or 50/50 biest ratio plus micronized progesterone in one transdermal cream — compounding logic, evidence limits, and endometrial safety.
metaDescription: Biest progesterone cream HRT: estradiol estriol 50/50 0.1% with progesterone 4%, compounded menopause therapy, and endometrial monitoring.
bodyHtml:
Biest (bi-estrogen) creams combine estradiol (E2) and estriol (E3), often in 50:50 or 80:20 ratios, with micronized progesterone 4% for transdermal menopausal hormone therapy. Estriol is a weaker estrogen thought to favor genitourinary tissue; estradiol provides systemic symptom relief.

Compounded bioidentical HRT is popular in functional medicine but lacks large RCT equivalence to FDA-approved patches. Endometrial protection requires adequate progestogen exposure; unmonitored bleeding warrants evaluation. Salivary hormone testing is not guideline-recommended for dosing.

CLYR Health offers biest + progesterone cream as a women's hormone preview SKU with prescriber-supervised monitoring.

sources.label: The 2022 NAMS Position Statement on hormone therapy.
FDA. Compounded bioidentical hormones: consumer advisory.
